description: Behavioral evals for the appstore-precheck skill: does the agent invoke the skill, run the scanner, reach the correct GREEN/YELLOW/RED verdict, and present it faithfully (Pierre trilingual one-liner + 2–3 sentence explanation per FAIL/WARN; scan output never paraphrased)? Inputs are self-contained throwaway iOS projects under evals/files/. The mechanical layer (scan.sh / verdict.sh / guard) is separately covered by the deterministic suite in tests/.

evals.name: red-blocks-submission
evals.prompt: I'm about to submit this iOS app to the App Store. Run the appstore-precheck and tell me whether I can submit.
evals.expected_output: A RED verdict that blocks submission: the metadata names another platform (2.3.10) and the paywall is missing Restore Purchases, Terms of Use, and Privacy Policy (3.1.2). No .precheck-pass token is written.
assertions: The verdict is RED and submission is reported as blocked
The .precheck-pass token is NOT written (it is removed/withheld)
Flags Guideline 2.3.10: the 'Android' / 'Google Play' reference in the metadata description
Flags Guideline 3.1.2: missing Restore Purchases, Terms of Use, and Privacy Policy on the paywall
Pierre explains each FAIL and WARN in 2–3 sentences (one block per finding; no random guideline sampling)
Pierre's trilingual block is visually separated: ### Pierre heading, bold language label + blockquote per language, --- between languages — not one compressed FR/EN line
The response opens with Pierre's trilingual block (French, English, then the user's conversation language; two blocks if the user already speaks French or English), then plain output
The scan's FAIL lines are shown verbatim (machine-faithful), not paraphrased or invented

evals.name: green-allows-submission
evals.prompt: Run the App Store pre-submission check on this project before I upload with fastlane.
evals.expected_output: A GREEN verdict: no FAILs. The paywall has Restore/Terms/Privacy and the metadata is clean, so the .precheck-pass token is written.
assertions: Confirms Restore Purchases, Terms of Use, and Privacy Policy are present on the paywall
The response opens with Pierre's trilingual block (### Pierre, bold labels, blockquotes, --- separators; two languages if the user already speaks French or English)

evals.name: no-iap-skips-paywall-checks
evals.prompt: Is this app ready for the App Store? Run the precheck.
evals.expected_output: No in-app-purchase signals are present, so the 3.1.2 paywall checks are skipped (not failed). The verdict is not RED.
assertions: Detects no in-app-purchase / subscription signals and skips the 3.1.2 paywall checks
Does NOT report any 3.1.2 paywall FAIL (missing Restore/Terms/Privacy)
